steps.title: Identify what blending genres prevents
steps.content: The strongest clue is that intergeneric forms, by definition, combine elements from different genres—fiction and essays, prose and poetry. When a work blends genres like this, it doesn't fit neatly into traditional single-genre categories.

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content: Unclassifiable directly names something that cannot be sorted into categories—exactly what happens when genres blend together. The other three contradict this idea (A and B assume clear traditional boundaries) or are unrelated (C concerns frequency). That matches choice D — D is correct.
